learn.title: Sharing Into 8 Equal Groups
learn.content: When you divide by 8, you share a total equally among 8 groups. Example: 48 counters shared into 8 groups → each group gets 6. So 48 ÷ 8 = 6.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.7 Lesson 7.8 'Divide by 8' pp.293-296
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic F — Division Facts: Divide by 8
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Halve Three Times Strategy
learn.content:
Dividing by 8 is the same as halving three times (because 8 = 2×2×2). Example: 24 ÷ 8
Step 1: 24 ÷ 2 = 12
Step 2: 12 ÷ 2 = 6
Step 3: 6 ÷ 2 = 3
Result: 24 ÷ 8 = 3. Check: 8 × 3 = 24 ✓

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.7 Lesson 7.8 'Divide by 8' pp.293-296
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic F — Division Facts: Divide by 8
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Array: 8 Rows
learn.content: A 8×9 array has 72 tiles arranged in 8 rows of 9. Reading it as division: 72 tiles in 8 rows means 72 ÷ 8 = 9 tiles per row. The ×8 fact 8×9=72 gives the ÷8 fact 72÷8=9.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.7 Lesson 7.8 'Divide by 8' pp.293-296
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic F — Division Facts: Divide by 8
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Number Line: Backward Jumps of 8
learn.content: To find 72 ÷ 8, start at 72 on a number line and hop backward by 8 each time: 72 → 64 → 56 → 48 → 40 → 32 → 24 → 16 → 8 → 0. Count the hops: 9 hops. So 72 ÷ 8 = 9.

learn.title: ÷8 검증 — 답 × 8 = 원래 수?
learn.content: ÷8 답을 구한 뒤 검산: 답 × 8 = 원래 수? 예: 56 ÷ 8 = 7 → 검산: 7 × 8 = 56 ✓ (= 7 × 2 × 2 × 2 = 14 × 2 × 2 = 28 × 2). 예: 64 ÷ 8 = 8 → 검산: 8 × 8 = 64 ✓ (정사각형 사실). 🔍 검증 기법: 답 × 8 = 답 더블의 더블의 더블 (예: 7→14→28→56). 흔한 실수 (M06): 절반 세 번 하다가 한 번 빼먹어 답이 두 배 큼 — 끝까지 셋 번 확실히.

practice_r2.question: Find the difference: 906 − 479.
practice_r2.explanation: 906−479: 6−9 borrow → 16−9=7; tens 9−7=2 (after lending across zero); hundreds 8−4=4. Result: 427.
